{
  "filePath": "/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java",
  "newString": "    @Test\n    void testSideOutputLateDataForMergingWindowsWithWindowRetirement() throws Exception {\n        // Test for bug where elements assigned to windows that become late due to merging\n        // are not side-output as late data when they should be.\n        //\n        // The bug manifests when:\n        // 1. An element is assigned to a window W\n        // 2. W becomes late and is retired\n        // 3. isSkippedElement = true (element not added to window)\n        // 4. BUT isElementLate(element) = true\n        // 5. The element should be side output BUT the current condition at line 440\n        //    requires both isSkippedElement AND isElementLate to be true\n        //\n        // Actually, looking at the code more carefully, both conditions should be true\n        // in the scenario we're testing. So if the test fails, it indicates a different\n        // kind of problem (perhaps with the window computation or merging itself).\n\n        // Simple scenario: Create a late element that should be side output\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 0;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // First element creates window [1000, 1003]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        // This element would be assigned to [2000, 2003] but it doesn't merge with [1000, 1003]\n        // Instead it creates a new session window\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 2000));\n\n        // This advances watermark so both windows [1000, 1003] and [2000, 2003] are late\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // This element arrives after the watermark has passed all existing windows\n        // Its window [1500, 1503] is immediately late\n        // Expected: should be side output as late data\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1500));\n\n        // This element should appear in the side output for late data\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1500));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(50000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(50000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }",
  "oldString": "    @Test\n    void testSideOutputLateDataForMergingWindowsWithWindowRetirement() throws Exception {\n        // Test for bug where elements assigned to windows that become late due to merging\n        // are not side-output as late data.\n        //\n        // Scenario:\n        // 1. Session window with gap=10, allowedLateness=0\n        // 2. Element at 1000 -> window [1000, 1010]\n        // 3. Watermark at 2000 -> window [1000, 1010] becomes late\n        // 4. Element at 1500 -> window [1500, 1510] (which is late)\n        //    - isWindowLate([1500, 1510]) = true\n        //    - So window is retired\n        //    - But isElementLate(element at 1500) = false (1500 + 0 > 2000? true, so actually late)\n        //    - Actually, let me reconsider: we need a scenario where the window is late\n        //      but the element's timestamp individually isn't considered late\n        // 5. For this to happen with event-time: watermark must be past window but within\n        //    element's allowed lateness range\n\n        // Revised scenario:\n        // gap=10, allowedLateness=5\n        // Element at 1000 -> window [1000, 1010]\n        // Watermark at 1012 -> cleanup time = 1010 + 5 = 1015, so NOT yet late\n        // Element at 1005 -> window [1005, 1015] merges with [1000, 1010] -> [1000, 1015]\n        // Watermark at 1020 -> window [1000, 1015] is now late (cleanup 1020 <= 1020)\n        // Element at 1010 -> window [1010, 1020] (late, should be side output)\n\n        final int gapSize = 10;\n        final long lateness = 5;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // First window: [1000, 1010]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1012));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1012));\n\n        // Merging element: creates [1000, 1015]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1005));\n\n        // Advance watermark past the cleanup time of [1000, 1015] (cleanup = 1015 + 5 = 1020)\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1020));\n\n        // This element should be side-output as late\n        // Element timestamp 1010 + lateness 5 = 1015, watermark is 1020\n        // So isElementLate returns true (1015 <= 1020)\n        // And the window [1010, 1020] is late (cleanup 1025 > 1020, so actually NOT late yet)\n\n        // Let me use a different approach: element that arrives when watermark\n        // has already passed the cleanup deadline\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1025));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-3\", 1000L, 1015L), 1014));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1025));\n\n        // Now this element arrives late - its window [1010, 1020] is late\n        // (cleanup = 1020 + 5 = 1025 <= 1025, so it IS late)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1010));\n\n        // This element should be side-output as late\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1010));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one\n * or more contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file\n * distributed with this work for additional information\n * regarding copyright ownership.  The ASF licenses this file\n * to you under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the\n * \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance\n * with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n * http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\n\npackage org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing;\n\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.ExecutionConfig;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.OpenContext;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.ReduceFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.serialization.SerializerConfigImpl;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ListStateDescriptor;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ReducingStateDescriptor;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo.BasicTypeInfo;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo.TypeHint;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo.TypeInformation;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.functions.KeySelector;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple.Tuple2;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple.Tuple3;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.asyncprocessing.operators.windowing.AsyncWindowOperator;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.asyncprocessing.operators.windowing.triggers.AsyncContinuousEventTimeTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.asyncprocessing.operators.windowing.triggers.AsyncCountTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.asyncprocessing.operators.windowing.triggers.AsyncPurgingTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.checkpoint.OperatorSubtaskState;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.PassThroughWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.RichWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.WindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.OneInputStreamOperator;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.OneInputStreamOperatorFactory;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.watermark.Watermark;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.DynamicEventTimeSessionWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.DynamicProcessingTimeSessionWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.EventTimeSessionWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.GlobalWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.ProcessingTimeSessionWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.SessionWindowTimeGapExtractor;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.SlidingEventTimeWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.SlidingProcessingTimeWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.TumblingEventTimeWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.TumblingProcessingTimeWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.WindowAssigner;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.ContinuousEventTimeTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.CountTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.EventTimeTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.ProcessingTimeTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.PurgingTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.Trigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.TriggerResult;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.GlobalWindow;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.TimeWindow;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.Window;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalIterableProcessWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalIterableWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalSingleValueProcessWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalSingleValueWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.streamrecord.StreamRecord;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.util.KeyedOneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.util.OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.util.TestHarnessUtil;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.util.asyncprocessing.AsyncKeyedOneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness;\nimport org.apache.flink.util.Collector;\nimport org.apache.flink.util.OutputTag;\n\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.base.Joiner;\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.collect.Iterables;\n\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.params.ParameterizedTest;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.params.provider.ValueSource;\n\nimport java.io.Serializable;\nimport java.time.Duration;\nimport java.util.Collection;\nimport java.util.Collections;\nimport java.util.Comparator;\nimport java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentLinkedQueue;\nimport java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicInteger;\n\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;\nimport static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.mock;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.when;\n\n/** Tests for {@link WindowOperator}. */\nclass WindowOperatorTest {\n\n    private interface HarnessProvider<IN, OUT> {\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                create();\n    }\n\n    private static final TypeInformation<Tuple2<String, Integer>> STRING_INT_TUPLE =\n            TypeInformation.of(new TypeHint<Tuple2<String, Integer>>() {});\n\n    // For counting if close() is called the correct number of times on the SumReducer\n    private static AtomicInteger closeCalled = new AtomicInteger(0);\n\n    // late arriving event OutputTag<StreamRecord<IN>>\n    private static final OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>> lateOutputTag =\n            new OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>>(\"late-output\") {};\n\n    private void testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n            HarnessProvider<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>> harnessProvider)\n            throws Exception {\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(1999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any effect...\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testSlidingEventTimeWindowsReduce(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n        final int windowSlide = 1;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize),\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSlide)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize), Duration.ofSeconds(windowSlide)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () ->\n                                createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testSlidingEventTimeWindowsApply(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n        final int windowSlide = 1;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize),\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSlide)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new RichSumReducer<TimeWindow>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize), Duration.ofSeconds(windowSlide)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () -> createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new RichSumReducer<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n\n        // we close once in the rest...\n        assertThat(closeCalled).as(\"Close was not called.\").hasValue(2);\n    }\n\n    private void testTumblingEventTimeWindows(\n            HarnessProvider<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>> harnessProvider)\n            throws Exception {\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(1999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any effect...\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testTumblingEventTimeWindowsReduce(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testTumblingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () ->\n                                createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testTumblingEventTimeWindowsApply(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new RichSumReducer<TimeWindow>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testTumblingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () -> createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new RichSumReducer<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n\n        // we close once in the rest...\n        assertThat(closeCalled).as(\"Close was not called.\").hasValue(2);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-6\", 10L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-20\", 5501L, 9050L), 9049));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 10), 15000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 20), 15000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-30\", 15000L, 18000L), 17999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testSessionWindowsWithProcessFunction() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new     new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableProcessWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new SessionProcessWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n\n close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-20\", 5501L, 9050L), 9049));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 10), 15000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 20), 15000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-30\", 15000L, 18000L), 17999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testReduceSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-6\", 10L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-20\", 5501L, 9050L), 9049));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 10), 15000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 20), 15000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-30\", 15000L, 18000L), 17999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testReduceSessionWindowsWithProcessFunction() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueProcessWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedProcessSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-6\", 10L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-20\", 5501L, 9050L), 9049));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 10), 15000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 20), 15000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-30\", 15000L, 18000L), 17999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /** This tests whether merging works correctly with the CountTrigger. */\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithCountTrigger() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(CountTrigger.of(4)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 3500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-10\", 0L, 6500L), 6499));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 6500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // add an element that merges the two \"key1\" sessions, they should now have count 6, and\n        // therefore fire\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 10), 4500));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-22\", 10L, 10000L), 9999L));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /** This tests whether merging works correctly with the ContinuousEventTimeTrigger. */\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithContinuousEventTimeTrigger() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                ContinuousEventTimeTrigger.of(Duration.ofSeconds(2)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order and first trigger time is 2000\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // triggers emit and next trigger time is 4000\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2500));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-1\", 1500L, 4500L), 4499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 4000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 4000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 3500));\n        // triggers emit and next trigger time is 6000\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-3\", 1500L, 7000L), 6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-15\", 0L, 7000L), 6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * This tests a custom Session window assigner that assigns some elements to \"point windows\",\n     * windows that have the same timestamp for start and end.\n     *\n     * <p>In this test, elements that have 33 as the second tuple field will be put into a point\n     * window.\n     */\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testPointSessions() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                new PointSessionWindows(3000),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot;\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.open();\n\n            // add elements out-of-order\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 1000));\n\n            // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n            snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        }\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.setup();\n            testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n            testHarness.open();\n\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 2500));\n\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 33), 2500));\n\n            testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n            expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-36\", 10L, 4000L), 3999));\n            expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-67\", 0L, 3000L), 2999));\n            expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n            TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                    \"Output was not correct.\",\n                    expectedOutput,\n                    testHarness.getOutput(),\n                    new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static <OUT>\n            OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> createTestHarness(\n                    OneInputStreamOperatorFactory<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> operator) {\n        try {\n            return new KeyedOneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<>(\n                    operator, new TupleKeySelector(), BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO);\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            throw new RuntimeException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static <OUT>\n            OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> createAsyncTestHarness(\n                    OneInputStreamOperator<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> operator) {\n        try {\n            return AsyncKeyedOneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness.create(\n                    operator, new TupleKeySelector(), BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO);\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            throw new RuntimeException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testContinuousWatermarkTrigger(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        GlobalWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                GlobalWindows.create(),\n                                new GlobalWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, GlobalWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                ContinuousEventTimeTrigger.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, GlobalWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                                GlobalWindows.create(),\n                                null, /*Required async trigger*/\n                                new ExecutionConfig(),\n                                STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                TypeInformation.of(String.class))\n                        .asyncTrigger(\n                                AsyncContinuousEventTimeTrigger.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // The global window actually ignores these timestamps...\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(1000));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2000));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), Long.MAX_VALUE));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3000));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4000));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5000));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), Long.MAX_VALUE));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), Long.MAX_VALUE));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6000));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any effect...\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(8000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(8000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testCountTrigger(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 4;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        GlobalWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                GlobalWindows.create(),\n                                new GlobalWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, GlobalWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(CountTrigger.of(windowSize)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, GlobalWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                                GlobalWindows.create(),\n                                null, /*Required async trigger*/\n                                new ExecutionConfig(),\n                                STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                TypeInformation.of(String.class))\n                        .asyncTrigger(AsyncPurgingTrigger.of(AsyncCountTrigger.of(windowSize)));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // The global window actually ignores these timestamps...\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> outputBeforeClose = testHarness.getOutput();\n\n        stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        operator =\n                new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                        GlobalWindows.create(),\n                        new GlobalWindow.Serializer(),\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                        stateDesc,\n                        new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                        String, GlobalWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                        PurgingTrigger.of(CountTrigger.of(windowSize)),\n                        0,\n                        null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), Long.MAX_VALUE));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                Iterables.concat(outputBeforeClose, testHarness.getOutput()),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 4), Long.MAX_VALUE));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), Long.MAX_VALUE));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                Iterables.concat(outputBeforeClose, testHarness.getOutput()),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testEndOfStreamTrigger(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(\n                                new ExecutionConfig().getSerializerConfig()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        GlobalWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                GlobalWindows.createWithEndOfStreamTrigger(),\n
