     // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2100));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(\"GOT: (key2,1)\", 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2100)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction2\n            implements WindowFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<String> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            out.collect(\"GOT: \" + Joiner.on(\",\").join(input));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 1;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncApply(new PassThroughFunction()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2000)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyReduceStateForTumblingWindows(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 1;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2000)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testSideOutputLateDataForMergingWindowsWithWindowRetirement() throws Exception {\n        // Test for bug where elements assigned to windows that become late due to merging\n        // are not side-output as late data.\n        //\n        // Scenario:\n        // 1. Session window with gap=10, allowedLateness=0\n        // 2. Element at 1000 -> window [1000, 1010]\n        // 3. Watermark at 2000 -> window [1000, 1010] becomes late\n        // 4. Element at 1500 -> window [1500, 1510] (which is late)\n        //    - isWindowLate([1500, 1510]) = true\n        //    - So window is retired\n        //    - But isElementLate(element at 1500) = false (1500 + 0 > 2000? true, so actually late)\n        //    - Actually, let me reconsider: we need a scenario where the window is late\n        //      but the element's timestamp individually isn't considered late\n        // 5. For this to happen with event-time: watermark must be past window but within\n        //    element's allowed lateness range\n\n        // Revised scenario:\n        // gap=10, allowedLateness=5\n        // Element at 1000 -> window [1000, 1010]\n        // Watermark at 1012 -> cleanup time = 1010 + 5 = 1015, so NOT yet late\n        // Element at 1005 -> window [1005, 1015] merges with [1000, 1010] -> [1000, 1015]\n        // Watermark at 1020 -> window [1000, 1015] is now late (cleanup 1020 <= 1020)\n        // Element at 1010 -> window [1010, 1020] (late, should be side output)\n\n        final int gapSize = 10;\n        final long lateness = 5;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // First window: [1000, 1010]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1012));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1012));\n\n        // Merging element: creates [1000, 1015]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1005));\n\n        // Advance watermark past the cleanup time of [1000, 1015] (cleanup = 1015 + 5 = 1020)\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1020));\n\n        // This element should be side-output as late\n        // Element timestamp 1010 + lateness 5 = 1015, watermark is 1020\n        // So isElementLate returns true (1015 <= 1020)\n        // And the window [1010, 1020] is late (cleanup 1025 > 1020, so actually NOT late yet)\n\n        // Let me use a different approach: element that arrives when watermark\n        // has already passed the cleanup deadline\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1025));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-3\", 1000L, 1015L), 1014));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1025));\n\n        // Now this element arrives late - its window [1010, 1020] is late\n        // (cleanup = 1020 + 5 = 1025 <= 1025, so it IS late)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1010));\n\n        // This element should be side-output as late\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1010));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyReduceStateForSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 1000L, 4000L), 3999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyStateNoResultForTumblingWindows(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 1;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n        org.apache.flink.api.common.state.v2.ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                windowStateDescV2 =\n                        new org.apache.flink.api.common.state.v2.ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                                \"window-contents\",\n                                STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new EmptyReturnFunction()),\n                                new FireEverytimeOnElementAndEventTimeTrigger(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        new FireEverytimeOnElementAndEventTimeTrigger(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncApply(new EmptyReturnFunction()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"test_key\", 1), 1000));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1699));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1799));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            // Be empty => list.get() is null\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get())\n                    .isNull();\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get())\n                    .isNull();\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[]\");\n        }\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1699));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1799));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2000)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    //  UDFs\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> in : input) {\n                out.collect(in);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class EmptyReturnFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {}\n    }\n\n    private static class SumReducer implements ReduceFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public Tuple2<String, Integer> reduce(\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> value1, Tuple2<String, Integer> value2) throws Exception {\n            return new Tuple2<>(value2.f0, value1.f1 + value2.f1);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class RichSumReducer<W extends Window>\n            extends RichWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, W> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private boolean openCalled = false;\n\n        @Override\n        public void open(OpenContext openContext) throws Exception {\n            super.open(openContext);\n            openCalled = true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void close() throws Exception {\n            super.close();\n            closeCalled.incrementAndGet();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                W window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n\n            assertThat(openCalled).as(\"Open was not called\").isTrue();\n\n            int sum = 0;\n\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> t : input) {\n                sum += t.f1;\n            }\n            out.collect(new Tuple2<>(key, sum));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple2ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    return sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple3ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    comparison = (int) (sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1);\n                    if (comparison != 0) {\n                        return comparison;\n                    }\n                    return (int) (sr0.getValue().f2 - sr1.getValue().f2);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class TupleKeySelector implements KeySelector<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public String getKey(Tuple2<String, Integer> value) throws Exception {\n            return value.f0;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class SessionWindowFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            int sum = 0;\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> i : values) {\n                sum += i.f1;\n            }\n            String resultString = key + \"-\" + sum;\n            out.collect(new Tuple3<>(resultString, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class ReducedSessionWindowFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> val : values) {\n                out.collect(new Tuple3<>(key + \"-\" + val.f1, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class SessionProcessWindowFunction\n            extends ProcessWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void process(\n                String key,\n                Context context,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            int sum = 0;\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> i : values) {\n                sum += i.f1;\n            }\n            String resultString = key + \"-\" + sum;\n            TimeWindow window = context.window();\n            out.collect(new Tuple3<>(resultString, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class ReducedProcessSessionWindowFunction\n            extends ProcessWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void process(\n                String key,\n                Context context,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            TimeWindow window = context.window();\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> val : values) {\n                out.collect(new Tuple3<>(key + \"-\" + val.f1, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class PointSessionWindows extends EventTimeSessionWindows {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private PointSessionWindows(long sessionTimeout) {\n            super(sessionTimeout);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        public Collection<TimeWindow> assignWindows(\n                Object element, long timestamp, WindowAssignerContext ctx) {\n            if (element instanceof Tuple2) {\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> t2 = (Tuple2<String, Integer>) element;\n                if (t2.f1 == 33) {\n                    return Collections.singletonList(new TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp));\n                }\n            }\n            return Collections.singletonList(new TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp + sessionTimeout));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * A trigger that fires at the end of the window but does not purge the state of the fired\n     * window. This is to test the state garbage collection mechanism.\n     */\n    private static class EventTimeTriggerAccumGC extends Trigger<Object, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private long cleanupTime;\n\n        public EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(long cleanupTime) {\n            this.cleanupTime = cleanupTime;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onElement(\n                Object element, long timestamp, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            if (window.maxTimestamp() <= ctx.getCurrentWatermark()) {\n                // if the watermark is already past the window fire immediately\n                return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n            } else {\n                ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n                return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) {\n            return time == window.maxTimestamp() || time == window.maxTimestamp() + cleanupTime\n                    ? TriggerResult.FIRE_AND_PURGE\n                    : TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void clear(TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) throws Exception {\n            ctx.deleteEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean canMerge() {\n            return true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onMerge(TimeWindow window, OnMergeContext ctx) {\n            ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"EventTimeTrigger()\";\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class FireEverytimeOnElementAndEventTimeTrigger\n            extends Trigger<Tuple2<String, Integer>, TimeWindow> {\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onElement(\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> element,\n                long timestamp,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void clear(TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) throws Exception {}\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     @Test",
        "     void testSideOutputLateDataForMergingWindowsWithWindowRetirement() throws Exception {",
        "         // Test for bug where elements assigned to windows that become late due to merging",
        "-        // are not side-output as late data.",
        "+        // are not side-output as late data when they should be.",
        "         //",
        "-        // Scenario:",
        "-        // 1. Session window with gap=10, allowedLateness=0",
        "-        // 2. Element at 1000 -> window [1000, 1010]",
        "-        // 3. Watermark at 2000 -> window [1000, 1010] becomes late",
        "-        // 4. Element at 1500 -> window [1500, 1510] (which is late)",
        "-        //    - isWindowLate([1500, 1510]) = true",
        "-        //    - So window is retired",
        "-        //    - But isElementLate(element at 1500) = false (1500 + 0 > 2000? true, so actually late)",
        "-        //    - Actually, let me reconsider: we need a scenario where the window is late",
        "-        //      but the element's timestamp individually isn't considered late",
        "-        // 5. For this to happen with event-time: watermark must be past window but within",
        "-        //    element's allowed lateness range",
        "+        // The bug manifests when:",
        "+        // 1. An element is assigned to a window W",
        "+        // 2. W becomes late and is retired",
        "+        // 3. isSkippedElement = true (element not added to window)",
        "+        // 4. BUT isElementLate(element) = true",
        "+        // 5. The element should be side output BUT the current condition at line 440",
        "+        //    requires both isSkippedElement AND isElementLate to be true",
        "+        //",
        "+        // Actually, looking at the code more carefully, both conditions should be true",
        "+        // in the scenario we're testing. So if the test fails, it indicates a different",
        "+        // kind of problem (perhaps with the window computation or merging itself).",
        " ",
        "-        // Revised scenario:",
        "-        // gap=10, allowedLateness=5",
        "-        // Element at 1000 -> window [1000, 1010]",
        "-        // Watermark at 1012 -> cleanup time = 1010 + 5 = 1015, so NOT yet late",
        "-        // Element at 1005 -> window [1005, 1015] merges with [1000, 1010] -> [1000, 1015]",
        "-        // Watermark at 1020 -> window [1000, 1015] is now late (cleanup 1020 <= 1020)",
        "-        // Element at 1010 -> window [1010, 1020] (late, should be side output)",
        "+        // Simple scenario: Create a late element that should be side output",
        "+        final int gapSize = 3;",
        "+        final long lateness = 0;",
        " ",
        "-        final int gapSize = 10;",
        "-        final long lateness = 5;",
        "-",
        "         ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =",
        "                 new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(",
        "                         \"window-contents\","
      ],
      "newLines": 24,
      "newStart": 3324,
      "oldLines": 32,
      "oldStart": 3324
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        "         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();",
        "         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();",
        " ",
        "-        // First window: [1000, 1010]",
        "+        // First element creates window [1000, 1003]",
        "         testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));",
        "-        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1012));",
        "-        expected.add(new Watermark(1012));",
        "+        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));",
        "+        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));",
        " ",
        "-        // Merging element: creates [1000, 1015]",
        "-        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1005));",
        "+        // This element would be assigned to [2000, 2003] but it doesn't merge with [1000, 1003]",
        "+        // Instead it creates a new session window",
        "+        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 2000));",
        " ",
        "-        // Advance watermark past the cleanup time of [1000, 1015] (cleanup = 1015 + 5 = 1020)",
        "-        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1020));",
        "+        // This advances watermark so both windows [1000, 1003] and [2000, 2003] are late",
        "+        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));",
        "+        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));",
        " ",
        "-        // This element should be side-output as late",
        "-        // Element timestamp 1010 + lateness 5 = 1015, watermark is 1020",
        "-        // So isElementLate returns true (1015 <= 1020)",
        "-        // And the window [1010, 1020] is late (cleanup 1025 > 1020, so actually NOT late yet)",
        "+        // This element arrives after the watermark has passed all existing windows",
        "+        // Its window [1500, 1503] is immediately late",
        "+        // Expected: should be side output as late data",
        "+        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1500));",
        " ",
        "-        // Let me use a different approach: element that arrives when watermark",
        "-        // has already passed the cleanup deadline",
        "-        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1025));",
        "-        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-3\", 1000L, 1015L), 1014));",
        "-        expected.add(new Watermark(1025));",
        "+        // This element should appear in the side output for late data",
        "+        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1500));",
        " ",
        "-        // Now this element arrives late - its window [1010, 1020] is late",
        "-        // (cleanup = 1020 + 5 = 1025 <= 1025, so it IS late)",
        "-        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1010));",
        "+        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(50000));",
        "+        expected.add(new Watermark(50000));",
        " ",
        "-        // This element should be side-output as late",
        "-        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1010));",
        "-",
        "         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();",
        "         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =",
        "                 testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);"
      ],
      "newLines": 30,
      "newStart": 3376,
      "oldLines": 35,
      "oldStart": 3384
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    public boolean intersects(TimeWindow other) {\n        return this.start <= other.end && this.end >= other.start;\n    }\n\n    /** Returns the minimal window covers both this window and the given window. */\n    public TimeWindow cover(TimeWindow other) {\n        return new TimeWindow(Math.min(start, other.start), Math.max(end, other.end));\n    }\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Serializer"
}

  "file_path": "/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java",
  "new_string": "    @Test\n    void testSideOutputLateDataForMergingWindowsWithWindowRetirement() throws Exception {\n        // Test for bug where elements assigned to windows that become late due to merging\n        // are not side-output as late data when they should be.\n        //\n        // Scenario:\n        // - Session windows with gap=100ms, allowedLateness=0\n        // - Element E1 at 1000 creates window [1000, 1100]\n        // - Element E2 at 200 creates window [200, 300]\n        // - Watermark advances to 400, making window [200, 300] late\n        // - Element E3 at 250 should be assigned to [250, 350]:\n        //   - Does it merge with [200, 300]? Yes, because 250 is within [200, 300]\n        //   - Does it merge with [1000, 1100]? No.\n        //   - After merge: [200, 350]\n        //   - Is [200, 350] late? Yes, because 350 + 0 <= 400\n        //   - Window is retired, E3 is not added\n        //   - isSkippedElement = true\n        //   - isElementLate(E3) = true (250 + 0 <= 400? yes)\n        //   - E3 should be side output!\n\n        final long gapSize = 100; // milliseconds\n        final long lateness = 0;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofMillis(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // First element creates window [200, 300]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 200));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(350));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(350));\n\n        // Advance watermark past cleanup time of [200, 300]\n        // cleanup = 300 + 0 = 300, watermark = 350, so window is late\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(400));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-1\", 200L, 300L), 299));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(400));\n\n        // This element should be side-output as late\n        // Window [250, 350] would merge with [200, 300] to [200, 350]\n        // [200, 350] is late (350 + 0 <= 400), so it's retired\n        // isSkippedElement = true, isElementLate(250) = true (250 + 0 <= 400)\n        // Should be side output!\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 250));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 250));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }",
  "old_string": "    @Test\n    void testSideOutputLateDataForMergingWindowsWithWindowRetirement() throws Exception {\n        // Test for bug where elements assigned to windows that become late due to merging\n        // are not side-output as late data when they should be.\n        //\n        // The bug manifests when:\n        // 1. An element is assigned to a window W\n        // 2. W becomes late and is retired\n        // 3. isSkippedElement = true (element not added to window)\n        // 4. BUT isElementLate(element) = true\n        // 5. The element should be side output BUT the current condition at line 440\n        //    requires both isSkippedElement AND isElementLate to be true\n        //\n        // Actually, looking at the code more carefully, both conditions should be true\n        // in the scenario we're testing. So if the test fails, it indicates a different\n        // kind of problem (perhaps with the window computation or merging itself).\n\n        // Simple scenario: Create a late element that should be side output\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 0;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // First element creates window [1000, 1003]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        // This element would be assigned to [2000, 2003] but it doesn't merge with [1000, 1003]\n        // Instead it creates a new session window\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 2000));\n\n        // This advances watermark so both windows [1000, 1003] and [2000, 2003] are late\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // This element arrives after the watermark has passed all existing windows\n        // Its window [1500, 1503] is immediately late\n        // Expected: should be side output as late data\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1500));\n\n        // This element should appear in the side output for late data\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1500));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(50000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(50000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }",
  "replace_all": false
}

  "newString": "    @Test\n    void testSideOutputLateDataForMergingWindowsWithWindowRetirement() throws Exception {\n        // Test for bug where elements assigned to windows that become late due to merging\n        // are not side-output as late data when they should be.\n        //\n        // Scenario:\n        // - Session windows with gap=100ms, allowedLateness=0\n        // - Element E1 at 1000 creates window [1000, 1100]\n        // - Element E2 at 200 creates window [200, 300]\n        // - Watermark advances to 400, making window [200, 300] late\n        // - Element E3 at 250 should be assigned to [250, 350]:\n        //   - Does it merge with [200, 300]? Yes, because 250 is within [200, 300]\n        //   - Does it merge with [1000, 1100]? No.\n        //   - After merge: [200, 350]\n        //   - Is [200, 350] late? Yes, because 350 + 0 <= 400\n        //   - Window is retired, E3 is not added\n        //   - isSkippedElement = true\n        //   - isElementLate(E3) = true (250 + 0 <= 400? yes)\n        //   - E3 should be side output!\n\n        final long gapSize = 100; // milliseconds\n        final long lateness = 0;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n             BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // First element creates window [200, 300]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 200));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(350));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(350));\n\n        // Advance watermark past cleanup time of [200, 300]\n        // cleanup = 300 + 0 = 300, watermark = 350, so window is late\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(400));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-1\", 200L, 300L), 299));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(400));\n\n        // This element should be side-output as late\n        // Window [250, 350] would merge with [200, 300] to [200, 350]\n        // [200, 350] is late (350 + 0 <= 400), so it's retired\n        // isSkippedElement = true, isElementLate(250) = true (250 + 0 <= 400)\n        // Should be side output!\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 250));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 250));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n   "oldString": "    @Test\n    void testSideOutputLateDataForMergingWindowsWithWindowRetirement() throws Exception {\n        // Test for bug where elements assigned to windows that become late due to merging\n        // are not side-output as late data when they should be.\n        //\n        // The bug manifests when:\n        // 1. An element is assigned to a window W\n        // 2. W becomes late and is retired\n        // 3. isSkippedElement = true (element not added to window)\n        // 4. BUT isElementLate(element) = true\n        // 5. The element should be side output BUT the current condition at line 440\n        //    requires both isSkippedElement AND isElementLate to be true\n        //\n        // Actually, looking at the code more carefully, both conditions should be true\n        // in the scenario we're testing. So if the test fails, it indicates a different\n        // kind of problem (perhaps with the window computation or merging itself).\n\n        // Simple scenario: Create a late element that should be side output\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 0;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // First element creates window [1000, 1003]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        // This element would be assigned to [2000, 2003] but it doesn't merge with [1000, 1003]\n        // Instead it creates a new session window\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 2000));\n\n        // This advances watermark so both windows [1000, 1003] and [2000, 2003] are late\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // This element arrives after the watermark has passed all existing windows\n        // Its window [1500, 1503] is immediately late\n        // Expected: should be side output as late data\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1500));\n\n        // This element should appear in the side output for late data\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1500));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(50000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(50000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }",

org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.GlobalWindow;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.TimeWindow;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.Window;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalIterableProcessWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalIterableWindowFunction;\nimport
